label: verify-54960-1
system:
You are the citation verifier in a legal-exploration pipeline. You are given one authority as cited by the pipeline's components, plus snippets showing how it was used. Using web search (prefer primary/official sources: leginfo.legislature.ca.gov, courts.ca.gov, law.justia.com, casetext.com, codes.findlaw.com, fppc.ca.gov), determine:
1. Does the authority exist?
2. Is the citation form correct (reporter cite, year, section number)?
3. Does it plausibly stand for what the snippets use it for?

Statuses: VERIFIED (exists, cite essentially right, usage consistent with what you found); MISCHARACTERIZED (exists but does not support the claimed use, or the cite attaches to the wrong case/section); NOT_FOUND (cannot locate after genuine searching); CONTESTED (real sources point in conflicting directions, or scope is genuinely unsettled); UNCLEAR (could not determine within a reasonable search).

You are NOT determining whether the authority is still good law (no Shepardizing) — say what you actually confirmed, no more. Be strict: a close-but-wrong reporter cite or a holding stretched past what sources show is MISCHARACTERIZED, not VERIFIED.
prompt:
## Authority as cited (variants seen in the documents)

- Cal. Gov't Code § 54960.1
- Gov. Code § 54960.1
- Gov. Code § 54960.1(d)
- § 54960.1
- § 54960.1(c)
- § 54960.1(d)

## How the documents used it

[delphi] …or the safe harbor; Cal. Gov't Code § 54959 (criminal 'knowing' violation) as distinct from civil enforcement under §§ 54960/54960.1 — verify whether civil claims under § 54960.1 require any showing of knowledge/intent; Cal. Gov't Code § 54960.1 (cure-and-correct demand procedure); Cal. Gov't Code § 54950 (declaration of purpose; liberal construction of the Act, narrow construction of exceptions); Wolfe v. City of Fremont — verify correct citation (panel split …

[tetlock] …ven that a majority already possesses the tainted information regardless of what happens procedurally going forward. |
| **SQ5**: What are the city's mandatory procedural deadlines and substantive response options under § 54960.1 for the served cure-and-correct demand (including consequences of an inadequate or absent response and exposure to a mandamus/validation action), and separately, what mental-state standard would the DA's public-integrit…

[dialectic] …ney General opinion(s) addressing email/listserv or other electronic sequential communications among a majority of a legislative body as serial meetings — locate correct citation before relying on the analogy.
- Whether Gov. Code § 54960.1's cure-and-correct procedure textually reaches a § 54952.2(b)(1) serial-meeting violation absent a completed vote, or whether general § 54960 relief is the correct vehicle at this pre-hearing stage.

### Antithesis — st…

## Context

The underlying question is a California public-agency hypothetical: q1 — The AI assistant as serial-meeting intermediary (Brown Act).

Verify this authority per your instructions.

canonical_cite.description: the correct full citation, if determinable

note.description: 1-3 sentences: what was confirmed or refuted, and the correct cite

text: {"status":"VERIFIED","canonical_cite":"Cal. Gov't Code § 54960.1 (Ralph M. Brown Act, \"cure or correct\" demand and mandamus/injunction procedure)","note":"§ 54960.1 exists and is the Brown Act provision letting a DA or interested person demand that a legislative body \"cure or correct\" specified action-taken violations before suing by mandamus/injunction to void the action. Subsection (c) sets the deadlines (90/30 days to demand, 30 days to cure, 15 days to sue) and (d) lists exceptions barring nullification (substantial compliance, debt/contract transactions, good-faith reliance, tax collection, adequate notice) — matching how tetlock and dialectic use the cite for deadlines, cure/no-response consequences, and scope. Notably § 54960.1(a) only covers violations of §§ 54953, 54954.2, 54954.5, 54954.6, 54956, or 54956.5 — it does not list § 54952.2 (serial meetings) — so dialectic's flagged question (whether 54960.1 textually reaches a bare 54952.2 serial-meeting violation, vs. general 54960) is a legitimate, well-founded scope concern rather than a misuse; likewise, the section contains no \"knowing\"/intent element (that language is unique to the criminal misdemeanor at §54959), so delphi's flagged open question is also accurate. No misuse found — the pipeline is correctly citing and appropriately hedging on genuine open scope/mental-state issues.","source_urls":["https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displaySection.xhtml?lawCode=GOV&sectionNum=54960.1.","https://codes.findlaw.com/ca/government-code/gov-sect-54960-1/","https://law.justia.com/codes/california/2021/code-gov/title-5/division-2/part-1/chapter-9/section-54960-1/"]}

data.canonical_cite: Cal. Gov't Code § 54960.1 (Ralph M. Brown Act, "cure or correct" demand and mandamus/injunction procedure)
data.note: § 54960.1 exists and is the Brown Act provision letting a DA or interested person demand that a legislative body "cure or correct" specified action-taken violations before suing by mandamus/injunction to void the action. Subsection (c) sets the deadlines (90/30 days to demand, 30 days to cure, 15 days to sue) and (d) lists exceptions barring nullification (substantial compliance, debt/contract transactions, good-faith reliance, tax collection, adequate notice) — matching how tetlock and dialectic use the cite for deadlines, cure/no-response consequences, and scope. Notably § 54960.1(a) only covers violations of §§ 54953, 54954.2, 54954.5, 54954.6, 54956, or 54956.5 — it does not list § 54952.2 (serial meetings) — so dialectic's flagged question (whether 54960.1 textually reaches a bare 54952.2 serial-meeting violation, vs. general 54960) is a legitimate, well-founded scope concern rather than a misuse; likewise, the section contains no "knowing"/intent element (that language is unique to the criminal misdemeanor at §54959), so delphi's flagged open question is also accurate. No misuse found — the pipeline is correctly citing and appropriately hedging on genuine open scope/mental-state issues.
